person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
"William de Say, granted his (probably dead) father's lands in a charter of the Empress Maud between Christmas 1141 and June 1142; joined his brother-in-law Geoffrey de Mandeville, 1st Earl of Essex of the 1140 creation, in rebellion against Stephen 1144; married Beatrice (died 19 April 1197 or some year before it), daughter of William de Mandeville, sister of 1st Earl of Essex and divorced wife of Hugh Talebot, and was allegedly killed in an attack on Burwell Castle, Cambs, along with his brother-in-law, 1st Earl of Essex Aug 1144, though he may well have died later." 

«b»Property

Manor of Saye's Court«/b»
"Gilbert de Magminot (or Maminot, as this name was afterwards more frequently spelt) fixed the scite of his barony here, which therefore was afterwards esteemed caput baroniæ, or head of the barony, and he erected a castle on it, as was usual in those days, every part of which has been long since buried in its own ruins; though some remains of stony foundations seem to point out the situation of it, near Saye's-court, in Bromfield, on the bank of the Thames, near the mast dock. His grandson, Wakelin, died without issue, in the third year of king Richard I. having been a good benefactor to the monks of Bermondsey, to whom, in the year 1157, he gave ten shillings rent, out of the mill of Deptford. On his death his sister Alice became his coheir, and brought this place, with much other inheritance, to her husband Geoffrey, second son of William de Saye, who granted this manor of West Greenwich (as it was then called) with the advowson of the church and its appurtenances to the Knights Templars, in pure and perpetual alms.

His son Geoffrey regained the possession of it by giving the Knights Templars that of Sadlescombe, in Suffex, for it. He ratified to the canons of Begham the lands of Brocele, which were part of his barony, and the church of St. Nicholas, at Greenwich, which his father had given to them. Geoffrey de Saye, last mentioned, being in arms against king John, with others of the barons, in the 17th year of that reign, his lands and fees, lying in Kent and elsewhere, were given to Peter de Crohun, though after the death of the king he was taken into favour, and his lands were restored in the 8th year of king Henry III.

«b»Birling Manor«/b»
"Ralph de Curva Spina, or Crookthorne, as his name was Englished, resided at Comport, or Comford-park, in the north-east part of this parish, where this family continued till the reign of king Henry II. when they were succeeded in their possessions here by that of Magminot. Walkelin de Magminot died without issue in the third year of king Richard I. anno 1191, and Alice, his sister became his heir, who carried this manor in marriage to her husband Geoffry, second son of William de Say, who in her right became likewise possessed of large estates at Deptford, Cowdham, and elsewhere in this county. His descendant, William de Say, died possessed of the manor of Birling, in the 23d year of king Edward I. holding it in capite by barony, and in some old deeds Birling is stiled caput baroniæ de Say, that is the capital seat of his barony."

zh.lifeSummary: 當Guillaume de SAY於1106年出生在法國時，他的父親Geoffrey de SAY22歲，母親Hawise FitzGilbert de Clare17歲。他於大約1134年與Lady Beatrice de Mandeville在英國艾可結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1180年8月4日在英國諾薩普頓史爾巴維爾享年74歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Guillaume de SAY于1106年出生在法国时，他的父亲Geoffrey de SAY22岁，母亲Hawise FitzGilbert de Clare17岁。他于大约1134年与Lady Beatrice de Mandeville在英国艾可结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1180年8月4日在英国諾薩普頓史爾巴維爾享年74岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: SAY
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Sai in Orne or Say in Indre, France, perhaps so called from a Gaulish personal name Saius + the Latin locative suffix -acum (see also French name 3 below).

English: nickname, perhaps for someone who made or sold say, a type of woollen cloth, or for an habitual wearer of clothes made of this material, from Middle English sai(e) ‘say’ (Old French saie, from Latin saga, plural of sagum ‘military cloak’). See also Sayer .

French (southern): topographic name from saix (from Latin saxum) ‘rock’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example, Say in Loire, Saix in Tarn and Vienne, Le Saix in Hautes-Alpes, or Les Saix in Isère.

variants.name: Sey
meanings: English and Scottish: probably a variant of   Say   particularly prevalent in Scotland.
Dutch and Flemish: from a short form of the ancient Germanic personal name Sigiheri (see Seger ).
West African (Ghana): Akan (Fante) name of unexplained etymology.

variants.name: Sayer
meanings: English: common occupational name from Middle English sayer, a phonetic variant of Sawyer , which in Wales and neighboring English counties may have been reinforced by the synonymous Welsh saer ‘carpenter’ and everywhere by a possible borrowing into Middle English of Old French seieor, saieur ‘sawyer’.

English: rare occupational name perhaps denoting an assayer of metals, or a servant who tasted his lord's food before serving, from Middle English saiour ‘assayer, tester’ (a shortened form of Old French assaiour, ultimately from Late Latin exagium, a derivative of exagmināre ‘to weigh’).
English: rare occupational name for a professional reciter or minstrel, from Middle English seier(e) ‘speaker’.

variants.name: Saye
meanings: Americanized form of Slovenian Saje: nickname for someone with very dark, sooty hair, derived from the root of sajast ‘sooty’, or directly from saja ‘soot’.
West African (Liberia): from the personal name Saye, found among the Mano (Mah) people, denoting a first-born boy.

nameMeanings.name: Guillaume
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Guillaume de SAY was born in 1106, in France, his father, Geoffrey de SAY, was 22 and his mother, Hawise FitzGilbert de Clare, was 17. He married Lady Beatrice de Mandeville about 1134, in Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 4 August 1180, in Barnwell, Northamptonshire, England, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Guillaume de SAY est né en 1106, à France, son père, Geoffrey de SAY, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Hawise FitzGilbert de Clare, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Lady Beatrice de Mandeville environ 1134, à Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 4 août 1180, à Barnwell, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 74 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Guillaume de SAY 1106 in Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Geoffrey de SAY 22 und seine Mutter Hawise FitzGilbert de Clare 17. Er hat um 1134 in Essex, England Lady Beatrice de Mandeville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 4. August 1180 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Barnwell, Northamptonshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Guillaume de SAY è nato il nel 1106 nel Francia, il suo padre, Geoffrey de SAY, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Hawise FitzGilbert de Clare, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato Lady Beatrice de Mandeville circa 1134, nel Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 4 agosto 1180, nel Barnwell, Northamptonshire, England, all'età di 74 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Guillaume de SAYが1106年にフランスで生まれた時，父Geoffrey de SAYは22歳，母Hawise FitzGilbert de Clareは17歳であった。約1134年にイギリスエセックスでLady Beatrice de Mandevilleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1180年8月4日にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアバーンウエルで74歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Guillaume de SAY(이)가 1106년 프랑스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Geoffrey de SAY(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Hawise FitzGilbert de Clare(은)는 17세였다. 그는 대략 1134년 영국 에세스에서 Lady Beatrice de Mandeville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1180년 8월 4일 영국 노르섬프토실르 바른웰에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Guillaume de SAY nasceu em 1106, em França, seu pai, Geoffrey de SAY, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Hawise FitzGilbert de Clare, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Beatrice de Mandeville aproximadamente 1134, em Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 4 de agosto de 1180, em Barnwell, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, com 74 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Guillaume de SAY родился в 1106 г. в местности Франция, его отцу по имени Geoffrey de SAY было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Hawise FitzGilbert de Clare, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Beatrice de Mandeville около 1134 г. в местности эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 4 августа 1180 г. в местности барнвелл, нортамптоншир, Англия в возрасте 74 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Guillaume de SAY nació en 1106, en Francia, su papá, Geoffrey de SAY, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Hawise FitzGilbert de Clare, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Lady Beatrice de Mandeville aproximadamente 1134, en Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 4 de agosto de 1180, en Barnwell, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 74 años.
